name: easylink

metadata.name: easylink
metadata.summary: Research repository for the EasyLink ER ecosystem project.

documentation:
# EasyLink

EasyLink is a framework that allows users to build and run highly configurable
entity resolution (ER) pipelines.

## Installation

There are a few things to install in order to use this package:

- Install singularity. If this is not already installed on your system, you will likely need to request it from your system admin. Refer to <https://docs.sylabs.io/guides/4.1/admin-guide/installation.html>

- Install graphviz via

  > `> conda install graphviz`

- Install EasyLink via

  > `> pip install easylink`
  >
  > OR
  >
  > `> cd <path/to/repositories/>`
  >
  > `> git clone git@github.com:ihmeuw/easylink.git`
  >
  > `> # OR git clone https://github.com/ihmeuw/easylink.git`
  >
  > `> cd easylink`
  >
  > `> pip install .`

## Quickstart

To run a pipeline, use `easylink run` from the command line and pass in the
paths to both a pipeline specification and an input data specification:

> `> easylink run -p <PIPELINE-SPECIFICATION> -i <INPUT-DATA-SPECIFICATION>`

There are several other optional arguments to `easylink run` as well;
for help, use `easylink run --help`

Note that a schematic of the pipeline's directed acyclic graph (DAG) that is run
is automatically generated. If this schematic is desired \_without\_ actually
running the pipeline, use `easylink generate-dag`:

> `> easylink generate-dag -p <PIPELINE-SPECIFICATION> -i <INPUT-DATA-SPECIFICATION>`

As before, refer to `easylink generate-dag --help` for information on other
options.

## Requirements

TBD

classes.name: easylink.implementation.Implementation

methods.name: easylink.implementation.Implementation.validate
documentation:
Validates individual Implementation instances.

This is intended to be run from the Pipeline validate method.

methods.name: easylink.implementation.Implementation._load_metadata
documentation: Helper methods #

A representation of an actual container that will be executed for a particular step.

Implementations exist at a lower level than Steps. This class
contains information about what container to use, what environment variables to set
inside the container, and some metadata about the container.

classes.name: easylink.implementation.NullImplementation
documentation:
A NullImplementation is used to represent a step that does not have an implementation.

For example, the IO steps in the pipeline schema do not correspond to implementations
but ImplementationGraph requires an "implementation" attribute with input and output slots
for each node.

classes.name: easylink.implementation.PartialImplementation
documentation:
A representation of one part of a combined implementation that spans multiple steps.

A PartialImplementation is what is initially added to the implementation graph when
a combined implementation is used (i.e. an implementation that spans multiple steps).
We initially add a node for _each_ step, which has as its "implementation" attribute a
PartialImplementation. Such a graph is not yet fit to run. When we make our second
pass through, after the flat (non-hierarchical) PipelineGraph has been created, we find the set of
PartialImplementation nodes corresponding to each combined implementation and
replace them with a single node with a true Implementation representing the combined
implementation.

functions.name: easylink.configuration.load_params_from_specification
documentation:
Gather together all specification data.

This gathers the pipeline, input data, and computing environment specifications
as well as the results directory into a single dictionary for insertion into
the Config object.

functions.name: easylink.configuration._load_input_data_paths
documentation: Create dictionary of input data paths from the input data yaml file.

functions.name: easylink.configuration._load_computing_environment
documentation: Load the computing environment yaml file and return the contents as a dict.

classes.name: easylink.configuration.Config

methods.name: easylink.configuration.Config.computing_environment
documentation: The computing environment to run on (generally either 'local' or 'slurm').

methods.name: easylink.configuration.Config.slurm
documentation: A dictionary of slurm configuration settings.

methods.name: easylink.configuration.Config.spark
documentation: A dictionary of spark configuration settings.

methods.name: easylink.configuration.Config.slurm_resources
documentation: A flat dictionary of the slurm resources.

methods.name: easylink.configuration.Config.spark_resources
documentation: A flat dictionary of the spark resources.

methods.name: easylink.configuration.Config._get_schema
documentation:
Validates the requested pipeline against supported schemas.

Notes
-----
This acts as the pipeline configuration file's validation method since
we can only find a matching schema if the file is valid.

We use the first schema that validates the pipeline configuration.
Setup Methods #

A container for configuration information.

This class combines the pipeline, input data, and computing environment
specifications into a single LayeredConfigTree object. It is also responsible
for validating these specifications.

classes.name: easylink.pipeline_schema.PipelineSchema

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_schema.PipelineSchema.validate_step
documentation:
Validates the pipeline configuration against this instance of pipeline schema.

Notes
-----
Below, we nest the full pipeline configuration under a "substeps" key of
a root hierarchical step because the root step doesn't exist from the
user's perspective and it doesn't appear explicitly in the pipeline.yaml.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_schema.PipelineSchema.configure_pipeline
documentation:
Configures the PipelineSchema and corresponding StepGraphs.

Notes
-----
This recursively updates the StepGraphs until all non-leaf nodes are resolved.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_schema.PipelineSchema._get_schemas
documentation: Creates all allowable schemas for the pipeline.

An abstraction of all possible allowable pipelines.

A PipelineSchema is a Step whose StepGraph determines all possible allowable
pipelines. The fundamental purpose of this class is to validate that the
user-requested pipeline to run conforms to an allowable pipeline.

classes.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline._validate
documentation: Validates the pipeline.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline._validate_implementations
documentation: Validates each individual Implementation instance.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline.snakefile_path
documentation: The path to the dynamically-generated snakefile.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline.write_target_rules
documentation: Writes the rule for the final output and its validation

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline.write_implementation_rules
documentation: Writes the rules for each implemented step.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline.write_config
documentation:
Write any configuration settings to the Snakefile.
Currently only applicable for spark-dependent rules.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline.Pipeline.get_validations
documentation: Get validator file and validation rule for each slot for a given node
Abstraction to handle pipeline specification and execution.

functions.name: easylink.steps.dev.test.rm_in_directory
documentation: This script just tests that the dummy containers can actually be iterated (each taking as input the
output of the previous). It is not part of the dummy containers themselves!
https://stackoverflow.com/a/185941/

classes.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph._get_combined_slots_and_edges
documentation:
Get all the input slots, output slots, and edges needed to construct the
combined implementation

Parameters
----------
combined_implementation
    Name of the combined implementation.
nodes_to_merge
    A list of nodes to combine.

Returns
-------
The set of InputSlots, OutputSlots, and EdgeParams needed to construct the combined implementation

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph._get_edges_by_slot
documentation:
Get all the edges corresponding to each input and output slot in the list of nodes to be
combined.

Parameters
----------
nodes_to_merge
    A list of PipelineGraph nodes to be combined.

Returns
-------
A tuple of dictionaries keyed by slot, with values for edges corresponding to that slot.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph._get_duplicate_slots
documentation:
Given a list of slots, return only those which have duplicate names or environment
variables.

Parameters
----------
slot_tuples
    A set of (step_name, slot) tuples to check for duplication.
slot_type
    "input_slot" or "output_slot"

Returns
-------
A set of (step_name, slot) tuples that have duplicate names or environment variables.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph.update_slot_filepaths
documentation: Fill graph edges with appropriate filepath information.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph.get_input_slots
documentation: Get all of a node's input slots from edges.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph.get_input_output_files
documentation: Get all of a node's input and output files from edges.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph.spark_is_required
documentation: Check if the pipeline requires spark resources.

methods.name: easylink.pipeline_graph.PipelineGraph.validate_implementation_topology
documentation:
Check that the subgraph induced by the nodes implemented by this implementation
is topologically consistent with the list of metadata steps.

The structure of the pipeline.

The PipelineGraph is a DAG composed of Implementations and their file dependencies.
It is created by "flattening" the PipelineSchema (a nested StepGraph) with parameters
set in the configuration.

classes.name: easylink.step.ConfigurationState

methods.name: easylink.step.ConfigurationState.get_implementation_graph
documentation: Resolve the graph composed of Steps into a graph composed of Implementations.

methods.name: easylink.step.ConfigurationState.get_implementation_edges
documentation: Propagate edges of StepGraph to ImplementationGraph.

A given step's configuration state.

A ConfigurationState defines the exact pipeline configuration state for a given step, including the
strategy required to get the implementation graph from the step. There are two possible configured
step states: "Leaf" and "Non-Leaf".

classes.name: easylink.step.LeafConfigurationState

methods.name: easylink.step.LeafConfigurationState.get_implementation_graph
documentation: Return a single node with an implementation attribute.

A concrete instance of ConfigurationState for a leaf node in the pipeline graph.

This corresponds to a leaf node in the pipeline graph that is implemented by a single implementation.

classes.name: easylink.step.NonLeafConfigurationState

methods.name: easylink.step.NonLeafConfigurationState.get_implementation_graph
documentation: Call get_implementation_graph on each subgraph node and update the graph.

A concrete instance of ConfigurationState for a non-leaf node in the pipeline graph.

This class type is selected when a step has a non-trivial step graph and has been configured
to use the step graph (e.g. through a configuration key in the pipeline specification yaml).

classes.name: easylink.step.Step

methods.name: easylink.step.Step._get_step_graph
documentation: Create a StepGraph from the nodes and edges the step was initialized with.

methods.name: easylink.step.Step.implementation_node_name
documentation:
Determines a sensible unique node name for the implementation graph.

This method compares the step node names with the step names through the step hierarchy and
uses the full suffix of step names starting from wherever the two first differ. For example,
loop steps may have multiple loops with the same implementation and step, e.g. "step_3" and "step_3_python_pandas".
If we have node names step_3_loop_1 step_3_python_pandas the resulting implementation node name
will be step_3_loop_1_step_3_python_pandas. If all the node names and step names match, we have not
introduced any step degeneracies with e.g. loops or multiples, and we can simply use the implementation
name.

The highest-level pipeline building block abstraction.

Steps contain information about the purpose of the interoperable elements of
the sequence called a *Pipeline* and how those elements relate to one another.
In turn, steps are implemented by Implementations, such that each step may have
several implementations but each implementation must have exactly one step.
In a sense, steps contain metadata about the implementations to which they relate.

methods.name: easylink.step.IOStep.get_implementation_graph
documentation: Add a single node to the graph based on step name.

methods.name: easylink.step.InputStep.set_configuration_state
documentation: Configure the step against the pipeline configuration and input data.

classes.name: easylink.step.HierarchicalStep
documentation:
An abstraction to represent a step that may contain substeps.

A HierarchicalStep can be a single implementation or several 'substeps'. This requires
a "substeps" key in the step configuration. If no substeps key is present, it will be treated as
a single implemented step.

classes.name: easylink.step.TemplatedStep

methods.name: easylink.step.TemplatedStep._update_slot_mappings
documentation:
Get the appropriate slot mappings based on the number of parallel copies
and the existing input and output slots.

A helper class to represent a step that potentially contains some multiplicity.

Some steps may contain a specified amount of multiplicity, such as a loop or parallel split.
This class is a helper class intended to be subclassed by discrete concrete instances
of these multiples according to a specified transformation rule and the user-specified configuration.

classes.name: easylink.step.LoopStep

methods.name: easylink.step.LoopStep._update_step_graph
documentation: Makes N copies of the iterated graph and chain them together according to the self edges.

methods.name: easylink.step.LoopStep._update_slot_mappings
documentation: Gets the appropriate slot mappings based on the number of loops and the non-self-edge input and output slots.
A LoopStep allows a user to loop a single step or a sequence of steps multiple times.

classes.name: easylink.step.ParallelStep

methods.name: easylink.step.ParallelStep._update_step_graph
documentation: Makes N copies of the template step that are independent and contain the same edges as the current step

methods.name: easylink.step.ParallelStep._update_slot_mappings
documentation: Gets the appropriate slot mappings based on the number of parallel copies and the existing input and output slots.
A ParallelStep allows a user to run a sequence of steps in parallel.

classes.name: easylink.step.ChoiceStep

methods.name: easylink.step.ChoiceStep.validate_step
documentation:
Validates the ChoiceStep.

Notes
-----
We update the step graph and slot mappings here in validation as opposed to
in set_configuration_state (as is done in TemplatedSteps) because ChoiceStep
validation happens prior to set_configuration_state and actually requires
the step graph and mappings.

We do not attempt to validate the subgraph here if the 'type' key is unable
to be validated.

methods.name: easylink.step.ChoiceStep.set_configuration_state
documentation:
Sets the configuration state for a ChoiceStep.

Notes
-----
We update the step graph and slot mappings validate_step as opposed to here
(as is done in TemplatedSteps) because ChoiceStep validation happens prior
this but actually requires the step graph and mappings.
A ChoiceStep allows a user to select a single path from a set of possible paths.

EasyLink

modules.name: easylink.utilities
documentation:
=========
utilities
=========

Utility functions.

functions.name: easylink.utilities.data_utils.modify_umask
documentation: Decorator to wrap umask modification before making directories

classes.name: easylink.graph_components.InputSlot
documentation: InputSlot represents a single input slot for a step.

classes.name: easylink.graph_components.OutputSlot
documentation: OutputSlot represents a single output slot for a step.

classes.name: easylink.graph_components.EdgeParams
documentation:
A dataclass representation of an edge between two nodes in a networkx graph.

Edges connect the output slot of the source node to the input slot of the target node.

classes.name: easylink.graph_components.StepGraph

methods.name: easylink.graph_components.StepGraph.step_nodes
documentation: Return list of nodes tied to specific steps.

methods.name: easylink.graph_components.StepGraph.steps
documentation: Convenience property to get all steps in the graph.

A graph of Steps, with edges representing file dependencies between them.

StepGraphs are contained as an attribute of Steps, with the highest level being
the PipelineSchema.

classes.name: easylink.graph_components.ImplementationGraph

methods.name: easylink.graph_components.ImplementationGraph.implementation_nodes
documentation: Return list of nodes tied to specific implementations.

methods.name: easylink.graph_components.ImplementationGraph.implementations
documentation: Convenience property to get all implementations in the graph.

A graph of Implementations, with edges representing file dependencies between them.

ImplementationGraphs are subgraphs of a PipelineGraph generated by a particular Step,
including the PipelineGraph itself.

classes.name: easylink.graph_components.SlotMapping
documentation: SlotMapping represents a mapping between a parent and child node at different levels of the nested pipeline schema.

functions.name: easylink.utilities.general_utils.handle_exceptions
documentation: Drops a user into an interactive debugger if func raises an error.

functions.name: easylink.utilities.general_utils.configure_logging_to_terminal
documentation:
Sets up logging to `sys.stdout`.

# Parameters

verbose

: Verbosity of the logger.

functions.name: easylink.utilities.general_utils._add_logging_sink
documentation:
Adds a logging sink to the global process logger.

# Parameters

sink

: Either a file or system file descriptor like `sys.stdout`.

verbose

: Verbosity of the logger.

colorize

: Whether to use the colorization options from {mod}`loguru`.

serialize

: Whether the logs should be converted to JSON before they're dumped
  to the logging sink.

functions.name: easylink.utilities.general_utils.exit_with_validation_error
documentation:
Exits the program with a validation error.

Parameters
----------
error_msg
    The error message to print to the user.

functions.name: easylink.utilities.general_utils.is_on_slurm
documentation: Returns True if the current environment is a SLURM cluster.

functions.name: easylink.runner.main
documentation:
Run the `command` passed in.

This function is intended to be accessed via the cli.py module's command
line interface functions.

# Arguments

command

: The command to run. Current supported commands include "run" and "generate_dag".

pipeline_specification

: The path to the pipeline specification yaml file.

input_data

: The path to the input data specification yaml file (not the paths to the
  input data themselves).

computing_environment

: The path to the specification yaml defining the computing environment to
  run the pipeline on. If None, the pipeline will be run locally.

results_dir

: The directory to write results and incidental files (logs, etc.) to.

debug

: If False (the default), will suppress some of the workflow output. This
  is intended to only be used for testing and development purposes.

functions.name: easylink.runner.get_singularity_args
documentation: Get the singularity arguments for the pipeline run.

functions.name: easylink.cli.easylink
documentation: The command line entrypoint to the EasyLink utility.

functions.name: easylink.cli.run
documentation: Run a pipeline from the command line.

functions.name: easylink.cli.generate_dag
documentation: Generate an image of the proposed pipeline DAG.

classes.name: easylink.rule.Rule

methods.name: easylink.rule.Rule._build_rule
documentation:
Builds the snakemake rule to be written to the Snakefile.

This is an abstract method and must be implemented by concrete instances.

Abstract class to define interface between Steps and Implementations.

This class is responsible for converting the defined interfaces between
Steps and Implementations to a Snakemake rule and writing it out to the
snakefile to be run.

classes.name: easylink.rule.TargetRule

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.TargetRule.target_files
documentation: List of file paths.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.TargetRule.validation
documentation: Name of file created by InputValidationRule.

A rule that defines the final output of the pipeline.

Snakemake will determine the DAG based on this target.

classes.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.name
documentation: Name to give the rule.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.step_name

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.implementation_name
documentation: Name of the implementation.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.input_slots
documentation: List of file paths required by implementation.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.validations
documentation: Names of files created by InputValidationRule to check for compatible input.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.output
documentation: List of file paths created by implementation.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.resources
documentation: Computational resources used by executor (e.g. SLURM).

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.envvars
documentation: Dictionary of environment variables to set.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.diagnostics_dir

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.image_path

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.script_cmd

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.ImplementedRule.requires_spark

A rule that defines the execution of an implementation

classes.name: easylink.rule.InputValidationRule

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.InputValidationRule.name
documentation: Name of the rule.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.InputValidationRule.slot_name
documentation: Name of the input slot.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.InputValidationRule.input
documentation: List of file paths to validate.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.InputValidationRule.output
documentation: File path to touch on successful validation. It must be used as an input for next rule.

class_variables.name: easylink.rule.InputValidationRule.validator
documentation: Callable that takes a file path as input. Raises an error if invalid.
A rule that validates input files against validator for an implementation or the final output
